name: Abdullah ibn Umayr
title: Lion of Karbala
shortDescription: A brave and well-built man who, upon seeing the Kufans mobilizing to fight the son of the Prophet, rushed to help the Imam with his wife.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn Umayr al-Kalbi was from the Bani 'Ulaym tribe and was a brave, muscular, and valiant Arab in Kufa. One day in Nukhayla of Kufa, he saw a massive army being organized. He asked: 'Where is this army going?' They replied: 'To fight Hussain, the son of the Prophet's daughter!'.

Abdullah said to himself: 'By God, I have always wished to fight the polytheists, but now I see that fighting those who attack the son of their Prophet is no less rewarding with God than fighting the polytheists'. He immediately went to his wife 'Umm Wahb' and expressed his intention. His wife, a pious and brave woman, not only didn't stop him but said: 'God has guided you to the best path, take me with you as well'.

They secretly left Kufa at night and reached Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan in Karbala. On the day of Ashura, when Umar Sa'd shot the first arrow, two warriors of the Kufan army (Yasar and Salim) stepped forward and challenged to single combat. Habib ibn Mazahir and Burayr wanted to go, but the Imam told them to sit.

At this moment, Abdullah ibn Umayr, a tall and muscular man, asked for permission. Imam Hussain (AS) looked at his strong arms and said: 'I think he is a suitable match for them'. Abdullah charged into the battlefield. The enemies, not recognizing him, said: 'Who are you? We want Habib and Zuhayr'. Abdullah angrily shouted: 'O sons of unchaste women! Is there anyone better than you to come to the battlefield?'

In a stunning hand-to-hand combat, Abdullah killed both Kufan warriors simultaneously with unparalleled bravery. After this victory, he returned to the battlefield in subsequent attacks, fighting courageously until he was martyred and his head was thrown towards the tents.
quoteArabic: يا حُسَيْنُ، إِنّي لَأَرْجو أَنْ يَكونَ غَزْوُ هؤُلاءِ الّذينَ يَغْزونَ ابْنَ بِنْتِ نَبِيِّهِمْ أَفْضَلَ مِن غَزْوِ المُشْرِكينَ
quote: Fighting those who attack the son of their Prophet is no less rewarding than fighting the polytheists.
